meta.source: English Grammar in Use 5th (inspired index, not a reproduction)
meta.note: Rough CEFR levels and learner-friendly descriptions for common units.

units.title: Present continuous (I am doing)
units.description: Talking about actions happening now or around now.

units.title: Present simple (I do)
units.description: Talking about routines, facts and general truths.

units.title: Present continuous and present simple 1
units.description: Choosing between present continuous and present simple for now vs. habits.

units.title: Present continuous and present simple 2
units.description: Verbs that are not normally used in continuous forms and other special cases.

units.title: Past simple (I did)
units.description: Talking about finished actions and events in the past.

units.title: Present perfect 1 (I have done)
units.description: Talking about life experiences and past actions with a result now.

units.title: Present perfect 2 (I have done vs. I did)
units.description: Contrasting present perfect and past simple for time reference.

units.title: Present perfect continuous (I have been doing)
units.description: Talking about activities that started in the past and continue or have an effect now.

units.title: Past continuous (I was doing)
units.description: Talking about an activity in progress at a moment in the past.

units.title: Present perfect vs. present perfect continuous
units.description: Differentiating finished results from ongoing activities.

units.title: Future forms 1 (will, going to)
units.description: Using will and going to for decisions, predictions and plans.

units.title: Future forms 2 (present continuous, present simple)
units.description: Talking about fixed arrangements and timetabled events in the future.

units.title: Used to (I used to do)
units.description: Talking about past habits and states that are no longer true.

units.title: Questions and negatives in the present
units.description: Forming basic questions and negatives in the present simple and present continuous.

units.title: Questions and negatives in the past
units.description: Forming basic questions and negatives with did and was/were.

units.title: Articles 1 (a, an, the)
units.description: Using a, an and the with countable nouns in easy situations.

units.title: Articles 2 (the, no article)
units.description: Deciding when to use the or no article for general and specific reference.

units.title: Countable and uncountable nouns
units.description: Using many, much, a few, a little and other quantity expressions.

units.title: Some and any
units.description: Using some and any in positive sentences, negatives and questions.

units.title: Pronouns and possessives
units.description: Using subject, object and possessive forms (I, me, my, mine, etc.).

units.title: Comparatives and superlatives
units.description: Comparing people and things with -er, more, the most, etc.

units.title: Too, enough and very
units.description: Using too and enough to talk about degree and quantity.

units.title: Adverbs of frequency and manner
units.description: Using adverbs like usually, often, quickly to describe habits and how actions happen.

units.title: Prepositions of time and place
units.description: Using at, on, in and common prepositions with places and times.

units.title: Present real conditionals (if + present, will)
units.description: Talking about possible future results of real conditions.

units.title: Zero conditional (if + present, present)
units.description: Talking about general truths and things that are always or usually true.

units.title: Modal verbs 1 (can, could, be able to)
units.description: Expressing ability, possibility and requests.

units.title: Modal verbs 2 (must, have to, should)
units.description: Expressing obligation, rules, advice and recommendations.

units.title: Requests and offers (can, could, would, shall)
units.description: Making polite offers, invitations and requests.

units.title: Gerunds and infinitives 1 (verb + -ing / to + verb)
units.description: Using verb patterns like enjoy doing, want to do, hope to do.

units.title: Gerunds and infinitives 2 (like to do vs. like doing)
units.description: Understanding meaning differences with -ing vs. to + verb after certain verbs.

units.title: Reported speech 1 (say, tell, ask)
units.description: Reporting statements and questions in a simple way.

units.title: Relative clauses 1 (who, which, that)
units.description: Adding information about a person or thing with relative pronouns.

units.title: Relative clauses 2 (whose, where, non-defining)
units.description: Using more advanced relative clauses in longer sentences.

units.title: Passive voice 1 (present and past)
units.description: Focusing on the object or result instead of the subject (is made, was built).

units.title: Passive voice 2 (present perfect, modals)
units.description: Using passive forms with have been and modal verbs.

units.title: Linking words (and, but, so, because)
units.description: Connecting ideas in a simple way to explain reasons and results.

units.title: More linking expressions (although, however, in spite of)
units.description: Contrasting ideas and making more complex connections between sentences.

units.title: Question tags (isn’t it?, don’t you?)
units.description: Checking information and keeping conversation going with small questions at the end.

units.title: Ellipsis and short answers
units.description: Giving natural short answers without repeating full sentences.
